response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished polyglot engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js and software craftsmanship credentials, but he fails to meet the two most critical mandatory requirements for this role. His C++ experience amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) plus a university thesis project, falling drastically short of the required 5–10 years of professional C++ on Windows. He has no capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domain exposure — his stint at RIDE Capital involved general fintech investment tooling, not trading systems or fixed-income instruments. No SQL Server or Oracle proficiency is evidenced, and Windows platform development is absent from his profile entirely.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and pairing practiced consistently across roles
Broad systems experience including low-latency micro-services (Dunnhumby), embedded/hardware (Altom), and high-throughput data pipelines
Demonstrated ability to pick up new domains and technologies quickly, evidenced by successfully taking over iOS/tvOS development at WatchUp
concerns: C++ experience is approximately 6 months professionally (NI LabWindows, 2014–2015) — far below the mandatory 5–10 year requirement
Zero capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domain knowledge; no exposure to trading systems, derivatives, or financial instruments
No SQL Server or Oracle experience mentioned; primary database exposure is MongoDB and MySQL, neither of which aligns with the mandatory SQL requirement
